title: Mark Spitz's Golden Splash
story: Splash! The US swim team raced to victory in the 4 x 100 m medley relay at the Munich Olympics on September 4, 1972. Mike Stamm, Tom Bruce, Mark Spitz, and Jerry Heidenreich powered through the water, setting a world record of 3:48.16. But the real magic happened when Mark Spitz touched the wall. He became the first athlete ever to win seven Olympic gold medals at a single Games. Talk about making waves!

title: A Bridge to Remember

title: America's First Airship Takes Flight
story: Floating like a giant silver cigar, the USS Shenandoah made history. On September 4, 1923, this massive airship became the first of its kind built in America to soar through the skies. Filled with helium gas, it was safer than earlier hydrogen-filled blimps. The Shenandoah marked the beginning of a new era in aviation, inspiring awe in all who saw it glide silently overhead.
activity_question: What type of gas was the USS Shenandoah filled with?
bonus_fact: The name 'Shenandoah' means 'daughter of the stars' in Native American language.

title: NASCAR's Thrilling Debut on Paved Track
story: Engines roared as NASCAR made history in 1950. The Southern 500 at Darlington Raceway marked the first-ever 500-mile race on a paved superspeedway. Drivers zoomed around the new track on September 4, pushing their cars to the limit. In a nail-biting finish, Johnny Mantz crossed the line first in his Plymouth, claiming victory in this groundbreaking event.
activity_question: What type of car did Johnny Mantz drive to victory?
follow_up_question: Why do you think NASCAR switched from dirt tracks to paved ones?

title: A Queen's Long Reign Ends
story: After ruling for nearly six decades, Queen Wilhelmina of the Netherlands stepped down from her throne in 1948. On September 4, she handed the crown to her daughter Juliana. Wilhelmina's 58-year reign was the longest of any Dutch monarch. Though illness led to her decision, she left behind a legacy of strength and leadership.
activity_question: Who did Queen Wilhelmina pass the Dutch throne to?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for a leader to know when to step down?
